_readme: ENGINE-INF REVISION-1/2/3 BUILD assumption block, ids ASM-2135..2144 (10 entries) — EMITTED by the Fable BUILD agent writer-5 (2026-07-13) for central registration by the coordinator WITH THE COMMIT; registry/assumptions.jsonl is NOT touched by this pass (no git actions, build-role constraint).

RANGE STATUS at emission: the task authorized >= 2125, but a concurrent REVISION of docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md (not touched by this pass) already references ASM-2122..2124 and ASM-2130; this block therefore starts at 2135 (verified free by repo-wide grep at emission: max id referenced anywhere is 2130). The coordinator may remap with a note per the ASM-2117 precedent.

Companion artifacts: docs/next/design/engine-inference-under-typing.md (design through R3); prior blocks asm-engineinf-1950-1969.json, asm-engineinf-r1-2034-2049.json (ids 2100..2112), asm-engineinf-r2-2113-2117.json, asm-engineinf-r3-2120-2124.json (2120..2121 used), and the original build block poc/engine-inference/asm-1990-2009.json. Tags: MEASURED | DERIVED | STIPULATED. This block states NO feasibility conclusion: the seen-frame numbers below are exploratory-forever (ASM-2104/2108) and the registered verdict belongs to the post-freeze holdout run.

assumptions.claim: BUILD-2 SCOPE AND MODULE-SPLIT CUSTODY: the R1/R2/R3 mechanics are built into poc/engine-inference/ as follows. engineinf_wn.py is the ENGINE-FREE core (WN31 parsing, ASM-1991/1992 item extraction, the ASM-2106 cell key) byte-moved (not re-authored) out of engineinf_lib.py; engineinf_lib.py re-exports it and carries everything engine- or scorer-adjacent. The holdout extractor extract_holdout.py is a separate entrypoint importing ONLY engineinf_wn and asserts mechanically at exit that neither twin_engine nor engineinf_lib entered sys.modules — making the ASM-2104 'does not import the engine or scorer' custody clause a checked property, not a promise. The builder does not freeze, does not commit, and does not run the confirmatory holdout.
assumptions.rationale: The original engineinf_lib imported the engine at module load, so no extractor importing it could satisfy the custody clause mechanically; a byte-moved split preserves the pinned parsing semantics while making custody assertable.
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/engine-inference/engineinf_wn.py; poc/engine-inference/extract_holdout.py (custody self-check at end of main); docs/next/design/engine-inference-under-typing.md §2.5 [R1]; ASM-2104

assumptions.claim: K-LEMMA ARM ENCODING (implements ASM-2101): the collapsed arms are compiled by build_klemma_arms into arms/klemma-dom/ and arms/klemma-union/ with ONE relation per lemma — URNs urn:kot-engineinf:klemdom:<lemma> and urn:kot-engineinf:klemun:<lemma> — each carrying [subPropertyOf urn:kot-engineinf:any:<lemma>] plus the collapsed domain/range set (dominant sense's set for -dom; agreement-or-nothing per slot for -union); the any-<lemma> coarse relations are inherited as byte-identical file copies; classes come from the shared classes/ dir unchanged. arm_relation returns the lemma relation for EVERY item of the lemma including excluded senses (a lemma-level source cannot mark coverage), and the collapsed arms receive the identical item record including the gold tag (constitutively constant in it). MEASURED at build: dominants break->urn:kernel-v1:break.interrupt, find->urn:kernel-v1:find.locate, friend->urn:kernel-v1:friend.person, make->urn:kernel-v1:make.cause (max summed index.sense tag_cnt over wn31Synsets, ties to smallest URN); union survivors: friend range C_person ONLY (break/find/make all-slots disagree or refuse) — exactly the design §1.3.1 DERIVED expectation.
assumptions.rationale: The matched pair must be a deterministic function of the pinned kernel module bytes with no new authoring; recording the build-derived dominants and survivors makes the collapse auditable against the design's own derivation.

assumptions.claim: C-SHUF ORBIT ENCODING (implements ASM-2114 mechanics + ASM-2120 frame): orbit member semantics — per lemma, senses URN-sorted; member assigns to the sense at index i the domain/range constraint set of the sense at index perm[i]; subPropertyOf and classDeclarations untouched. Canonical enumeration: per-lemma itertools.permutations in lexicographic order (identity first), lemmas alphabetical, itertools.product order; MEMBER 0 IS THE IDENTITY (= K). The runner evaluates every member on ONE REPRESENTATIVE ITEM PER outcome-equivalence CELL (exact under ASM-2106 determinism; TBoxes cached by notes-stripped semantic sha) and emits per-(member,cell) orbit rows carrying verdict/refusal/derived_sides/gold_verdict; A_union and the calibrated p are computed at ANALYSIS from exactly those rows. MEASURED at build: |Orbit| = 960 = 5!*2!*2!*2!; 80 distinct TBoxes among 960 members (tie members correctly retained, e.g. break's three identical 'range material' sets); the identity member reproduces the pinned kernel module semantically (orbit-manifest identity_reproduces_k = true). The ASM-1996 single-rotation kshuf ARM is retired: its arms/kshuf/ directory is deleted at compile and K-shuf is no longer a scored arm.
assumptions.rationale: A frozen enumeration convention with identity-first indexing is what makes 'member 0 = K' checkable at analysis (the identity-vs-K consistency gate) and the randomization p reproducible from pinned rows alone.

assumptions.claim: RUNNER CUSTODY MECHANICS (implements ASM-2104 clauses 3/4): engineinf_runner.py refuses in EVERY mode (including --dry-plan) if any pinned H item id appears in any results artifact (exit 3, substring scan over results/**.json[l] for the quoted ids of holdout/items-h.json). --holdout additionally refuses, in order: (exit 3) if H rows pre-exist anywhere under results/; (exit 4) if the coordinator's freeze-marker file poc/engine-inference/FREEZE-AUTHORIZED does not exist — the marker is created only at freeze, so a pre-freeze confirmatory run is mechanically impossible, not merely forbidden. In --holdout the H divergence certificates (divergence-certificate-h.json) are computed from the engine pass and written BEFORE any scoring occurs in the same process. The registered analysis contract adds orbit_rows_path/orbit_rows_sha256 to the pinned stdin artifacts.
assumptions.rationale: The design demands custody 'mechanically enforced, not promised'; encoding the refusals as ordered exit codes makes each one separately demonstrable by the pilot (PC-7) at $0.
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/engine-inference/engineinf_runner.py (scan_h_contamination, refuse, --holdout path); analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py load_pinned; docs/next/design/engine-inference-under-typing.md §2.5 custody [R1]

assumptions.claim: HOLDOUT EXTRACTOR OPERATIONALISATIONS (implements ASM-2104 construction): (a) SemCor pin = data/semcor30/semcor.zip, sha256 126fa2e829ab63edd5b3fd9de45ef1d60d6880e01e25abc55b5ac7918a824655 (NLTK XML conversion of SemCor 3.0, brown1/brown2/brownv; primary Mihalcea mirror TLS-unreachable at pin time, disclosed in the corpus manifest); the extractor verifies the sha before consuming any byte. (b) Sentence reconstruction: <wf>/<punc> tokens in order, wf text with underscores expanded, joined by spaces; tagged occurrence = wf with lemma in the target set, lexsn ss-type matching the kernel POS; sense key lemma%lexsn mapped to a WN3.1 offset via the pinned index.sense (unmappable -> logged). (c) ANCHOR CHECK: the ASM-1991 parse of the reconstructed sentence must match the predicate at exactly the tagged token's index (per-wf token ledger mirroring the parse tokenization), else 'anchor-mismatch' exclusion. (d) Item ids hi-<lemma>-<kind>-<offset>-<seq> (scan-order seq) / hi-<lemma>-anomaly-<offset>-x-<noun>; decoy ids dc-*. (e) Decontamination excludes any (gold synset, object noun) pair present in ANY seen item; the novel-cell flag h_star = cell key (ASM-2106) absent from all seen items. (f) KILL-e1 ops rule, outcome-free: kind attested exercises O1, kind anomaly exercises O2; the (G1∪G3)_H* frame can span at most {O1,O2} and needs both kinds present. (g) The extractor also emits the PC-6 decoy bank (draw/hold/cut; verified outside Stage-A and the kernel-v0 panel) via the same code path.
assumptions.rationale: Every free constant the R1 holdout construction left open is fixed here mechanically so the extraction is reproducible from pinned bytes alone and the KILL-e1 count needs no outcome.

assumptions.claim: HOLDOUT EXTRACTION CENSUS + KILL-e1 RESULT (a COUNT of pinned items, no outcome computed anywhere): items-H = 1182 (H1 attested 145, H2 constructed-anomaly 3, H3 refusal 1034), of which 386 are novel-cell (h_star). The BINDING frame (G1∪G3)_H* = 91 items over 17 cells, spanning 4 lemmas but ONLY op O1 (attested) — the 3 H2 anomaly items all fall in seen cells (h_star false). KILL-e1 (>=30 items PASS at 91; >=12 cells PASS at 17; >=2 lemmas PASS at 4; >=2 ops FAIL at 1) therefore FIRED. Mechanism, measured: the pre-registered H2 unanimity base (seen gloss-example objects UNION H1 objects, ASM-2104) breaks side-unanimity for 11 of 13 minted synsets on real SemCor objects (logged 'g3-mixed-attested-sides'), leaving only two make synsets unanimous, whose cross-pairs land in already-seen cells. Exclusions logged: 1117 total (882 pronoun-argument, 83 no-nominal-argument, 50 anchor-mismatch, 50 decontaminated-seen-pair, 30 side-undetermined, 11 g3-mixed-attested-sides, 10 unmappable-sense-key, 1 no-lemma-match). Per ASM-2105 the freeze DOES NOT PROCEED; the pre-registered consequence is construction/inventory extension (E2) first. NOTE [DERIVED]: items/cells/lemma criteria comfortably pass — the sole blocker is the anomaly leg of the frame.
assumptions.rationale: ASM-2110 named H1 yield as the open risk with the KILL-e1 count as its $0 resolution path; the count resolved AGAINST freeze on the ops-span criterion, and recording the measured mechanism (unanimity erosion under the pinned rule) gives the designer exactly the datum a construction amendment needs.

assumptions.claim: ANALYSIS CONTRACT v2 (implements ASM-2115/2114/2120 verbatim): analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py consumes ONLY the sha-pinned rows + orbit-rows artifacts named by eligible stdin run records. Frame semantics: rows carry frame ('H' registered / 'seen' exploratory) and h_star; the binding frame is the h_star G1∪G3 cells; a seen-frame input runs the identical machinery and stamps exploratory_nonbinding=true. Gates recomputed from bytes: within-cell outcome-equivalence (fail closed), oracle=1.0, orbit-identity-vs-K-arm consistency on every frame cell (fail closed). Registered outputs include ep_a_delta_dom/_union, ep_b_delta, per-endpoint wins/losses/ties and net-win lemma spans, dist_span_ok, exact K correctness floors, kill_e1/e2a/e2b, cshuf_orbit_p/cshuf_rank/cshuf_active_n over A_union, no_net_harm margins, band_pass_affirm/band_fail_deflate, CONTENT-INERT flag, and the honesty secondary. The former LB95/bootstrap fields and the INSUFFICIENT-CLUSTERS rule are REMOVED per ASM-2115; output-fields.json is updated accordingly, and the mock validator drives the binding path with synthetic PASS/DEFLATE fixtures (incl. a synthetic 960-member orbit) through the real subprocess contract.
assumptions.rationale: The registered analysis must be a pure function of pinned artifacts implementing the R2/R3 decision rule with no residual inferential gates; the mock validator proves both bands and the content-inert cap are reachable before anything freezes.
assumptions.backing_ref: analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py; poc/engine-inference/output-fields.json; poc/engine-inference/mock_validation.py + results/mock-validation.json (mock_pass true, 15/15 checks)

assumptions.claim: PC-4' SENSE-TAG CANARY REMAP RULE: the canary remaps each pilot-slice item's gold synset to the nearest cyclically-next synset in the lemma's sorted minted∪excluded synset list WHOSE K-ROUTING TARGET (minted sense URN or NONE) DIFFERS from the current one. Rationale for the target-difference clause: a naive next-synset remap can land on a sibling synset of the SAME concept (e.g. break.interrupt's two synsets) or map excluded->excluded (None->None), where K's projection is legitimately unchanged and the canary would test nothing; requiring a changed routing target preserves the check's two-directional teeth exactly as ASM-2101/2111 intend (per-lemma arms payload-byte-identical under the remap; K and B-wn relation changed on every remapped item). Both directions PASS in the re-run pilot.
assumptions.rationale: The design pinned the canary's intent but not its remap constant; this fixes the one free choice mechanically and records why the naive constant is insufficient.

assumptions.claim: BLOCKING PILOT RE-RUN RESULT (instrument-validity only, never campaign evidence; supersedes ASM-2002 for freeze purposes per ASM-2111): PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS on the 17-item slice, mode REAL, $0 — PC-1 pass (all 5 baselines strictly between 0 and 1; refusal caps met); PC-2' pass (|Div(K,D-word-dom)|=16, |Div(K,B-wn)|=14 on the slice, >=2 ops and >=2 lemmas; EP-A existence on the full exploratory frame: |Div_dec(K,K-lemma-dom)∩(G1∪G3)| = 20 items, union 25 — both >= 3); PC-3' pass (960 = factorial product; identity reproduces K; members byte-differ; A_union computed and invariant under a relabeled (group-composed) enumeration; calibrated p computable end-to-end on the slice, value 0.15 — slice mechanics only; D-union not row-identical to D-dom); PC-4' pass (planted range-flip on break.malfunction changed exactly the 3 predicted rows; poisoned-gold canary silent on the world compiler AND the K-lemma/orbit module compilers; planted gold-reading compiler trips it; provenance G2-independent incl. klemma modules; sense-tag canary both directions); PC-5 pass (yield 0.978; oracle 1.0; double-run sha match; B-wn frames 73 typed); PC-6 pass (325 decoy items for draw/hold/cut run end-to-end through pin->mapping->extraction->gold->compile->engine->score, quarantined to results/decoy/, K fail-closed REFUSE on all, decoys verified outside Stage-A and the kernel-v0 panel, exclusions logged); PC-7 pass (results scan clean of H ids; items-H/gold-H sha-pinned with no rows; runner refusal demonstrated on a planted fake H row in BOTH modes (exit 3) and without the freeze marker (exit 4); KILL-e1 count cross-checked against the extractor record). FLAGS: kernel_as_text_na (structural, per ASM-1963) and kill_e1_fired_pre_freeze (the ASM-2140 freeze-gate fact, reported through the pilot; NOT a pilot-validity failure).
assumptions.rationale: Per the blocking-pilot protocol the revised instrument's every new interface (collapse compilers, orbit, holdout machinery, custody assertions) had to be exercised with teeth pre-freeze; all seven checks pass and the two flags are the disclosed, pre-registered kind.

assumptions.claim: EXPLORATORY SEEN-FRAME EXACT-CENSUS RECORD (quarantined; exploratory-forever per ASM-2104; mechanics validation of the R2/R3 analysis, NEVER a verdict — the ASM-2108 narrowed sentence remains the only licensed exploratory sentence, and its numbers reproduce exactly under the corrected scorer: K 46, B-wn 27, D-word-dom 26, D-word-union 21 of 56, oracle 56/56; new collapsed arms measure K-lemma-dom 26/56 and K-lemma-union 21/56 item-level): at CELL level on the seen (G1∪G3) frame of 56 items = 19 cells — EP-A dom: 6 divergent cells, exact delta +1.00 (6 wins / 0 losses); EP-A union: 5 cells, +1.00 (5/0); EP-B: 7 cells, +0.714 (6 wins / 1 loss); K exact correctness 1.00 on the EP-A union-frame and 0.857 on the EP-B frame; DIST-SPAN FAILS on all three endpoints (every net win-cell is a break cell); no-net-harm margins all 0; C-SHUF on the seen frame: |A_union| = 11 cells, T(identity) = 1.0, rank 1, calibrated p = 48/960 = 0.05 (ties in >=; passes the <= 0.05 gate exactly at the boundary); KILL-e2a/e2b do NOT fire on the seen frame (6 and 7 divergent cells respectively). Whole-bank census correctness: K 0.953, K-lemma-union 0.830, D-word-union 0.835, B-wn 0.792, D-word-dom 0.538, K-lemma-dom 0.476; honesty penalty sums K 10.0 (best), D-word-union 35.0, K-lemma-union 36.0, B-wn 44.0, D-word-dom 98.0, K-lemma-dom 111.0. Double-run sha match true; decision payload sha in run-result.json.
assumptions.rationale: The re-pinned instrument's exploratory pass is recorded once with hashes so later references are checkable and stay inside the ASM-2108 language cap; the DIST-SPAN failures and the boundary C-SHUF p on the seen frame are exactly the robustness information the binding gates exist to demand of the (still unrun) holdout.
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/engine-inference/results/rows.jsonl + orbit-rows.jsonl + run-result.json (pins + determinism sha inside); analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py output over the seen-frame pins (exploratory_nonbinding true) sha256:c193c5cd8855050fac5832d245683a2ba2643987f4f43be30f4f7bcd6295de06
